agent.system_template:
You are controlling a robot in a real indoor environment (a photorealistic 3D scan of a building). You interact only through these tools:

- observe(): look through the robot's forward-facing camera (RGB image plus a clearance readout: meters to the nearest obstacle in the left/center/right thirds of the view; 10.0 = open).
- step(actions): execute movement actions in order. 0 = STOP (permanently ends the episode — declares you have reached the goal), 1 = move forward 0.25 m, 2 = turn left 15 degrees, 3 = turn right 15 degrees.
- look_around(): one call returning four labeled views (ahead / right / behind / left); rotates 360 degrees and restores your heading (costs 24 turn steps).

Your task is to follow this navigation instruction to its endpoint:

"{{ task }}"

Rules:
- Alternate observing and stepping: look, decide where the instruction wants you to go next, move, look again.
- You have a budget of {{ step_budget }} movement actions; each step() result reports roughly how many remain.
- You succeed only if you issue action 0 (STOP) while within 3 meters of the instruction's endpoint. STOP is permanent — issue it only when you believe you are at the goal.
- Turning in place (e.g. step([2,2,2,2,2,2])) is a cheap way to look around when unsure.
- Work autonomously until you stop; nobody can answer questions.
agent.instance_template: Begin navigating. Call observe() first to see where you are.

tools.name: observe
tools.description:
Look through the robot's forward-facing camera.

Returns the current egocentric RGB view plus a clearance readout: distance in meters to the nearest obstacle in the left/center/right thirds of the view (10.0 = open, 10 m or more). If the goal object is centered ahead, clearance "center" is your true distance to it. Pure read — does not advance the simulator or consume step budget.

tools.name: look_around
tools.description:
Scan the surroundings in ONE call: rotates a full 360 degrees and
    returns four labeled views — ahead (0°), right (+90°), behind (+180°),
    left (+270°) — then restores the original heading exactly.

    Costs 24 low-level turn steps from the step budget but only one tool
    call. Use this at junctions and decision points instead of turning 15
    degrees at a time; your position and final heading are unchanged.

tools.name: step
tools.description:
Execute a sequence of movement actions, in order.

Actions: 0 = STOP (permanently ENDS the episode — issue it only when you believe the robot is within 3 meters of the goal), 1 = move forward 0.25 m, 2 = turn left 15 degrees, 3 = turn right 15 degrees.

Executes sequentially and halts early if the episode ends. Returns how many actions ran, total steps taken, remaining budget, and whether the episode is over. The camera view changes after stepping — call observe() to see the result. Note: when plenty of budget remains, your FIRST STOP request is withheld pending a placement check — call step([0]) again to confirm and execute it.

messages.content:
You are controlling a robot in a real indoor environment (a photorealistic 3D scan of a building). You interact only through these tools:

- observe(): look through the robot's forward-facing camera (RGB image plus a clearance readout: meters to the nearest obstacle in the left/center/right thirds of the view; 10.0 = open).
- step(actions): execute movement actions in order. 0 = STOP (permanently ends the episode — declares you have reached the goal), 1 = move forward 0.25 m, 2 = turn left 15 degrees, 3 = turn right 15 degrees.
- look_around(): one call returning four labeled views (ahead / right / behind / left); rotates 360 degrees and restores your heading (costs 24 turn steps).

Your task is to follow this navigation instruction to its endpoint:

"Turn right and walk along the red carpet to your right. Walk past the doorway wait by the glass info panes. "

Rules:
- Alternate observing and stepping: look, decide where the instruction wants you to go next, move, look again.
- You have a budget of 500 movement actions; each step() result reports roughly how many remain.
- You succeed only if you issue action 0 (STOP) while within 3 meters of the instruction's endpoint. STOP is permanent — issue it only when you believe you are at the goal.
- Turning in place (e.g. step([2,2,2,2,2,2])) is a cheap way to look around when unsure.
- Work autonomously until you stop; nobody can answer questions.
Begin navigating. Call observe() first to see where you are.
I'll start by observing my surroundings.

message.content: I'll start by observing my surroundings.

messages.content: I can see a red carpet ahead. The instruction says to turn right and walk along the red carpet. Let me look around to understand the layout better.

message.content: I can see a red carpet ahead. The instruction says to turn right and walk along the red carpet. Let me look around to understand the layout better.

messages.content:
Interesting. Looking at the views:
- Ahead: the long room with red carpet extending forward
- Right/Left: doorway with wooden panels (very close, 0.6-0.8m)
- Behind: a red carpet leads to what appears to be glass info panes/display cases mounted on the wall

The "behind" view shows glass display panels (info panes) with the red carpet leading toward them. The instruction says "turn right and walk along the red carpet... walk past the doorway wait by the glass info panes."

The red carpet ahead is the main one. Let me interpret this carefully. The glass info panes are visible behind me. But the instruction says turn RIGHT first. Let me look at the current heading and consider the red carpet to my right.

Let me observe the behind view more — that has the glass info panes which is the endpoint. The carpet ahead is the main long carpet. Let me first turn to face the red carpet path. The behind view is most promising for the endpoint (glass info panes).

Let me turn around to face the glass info panes area.

message.content:
Interesting. Looking at the views:
- Ahead: the long room with red carpet extending forward
- Right/Left: doorway with wooden panels (very close, 0.6-0.8m)
- Behind: a red carpet leads to what appears to be glass info panes/display cases mounted on the wall

The "behind" view shows glass display panels (info panes) with the red carpet leading toward them. The instruction says "turn right and walk along the red carpet... walk past the doorway wait by the glass info panes."

The red carpet ahead is the main one. Let me interpret this carefully. The glass info panes are visible behind me. But the instruction says turn RIGHT first. Let me look at the current heading and consider the red carpet to my right.

Let me observe the behind view more — that has the glass info panes which is the endpoint. The carpet ahead is the main long carpet. Let me first turn to face the red carpet path. The behind view is most promising for the endpoint (glass info panes).

Let me turn around to face the glass info panes area.

messages.content:
Now I'm facing the area with glass info panes (the illuminated display cases mounted on the wall to the right). I can see the red carpet leading forward. There's a doorway on the left side. 

The instruction: "Turn right and walk along the red carpet to your right. Walk past the doorway, wait by the glass info panes."

I can see the glass info panes ahead-right (the lit display cases). Let me move forward along the red carpet toward them, passing the doorway.

message.content:
Now I'm facing the area with glass info panes (the illuminated display cases mounted on the wall to the right). I can see the red carpet leading forward. There's a doorway on the left side. 

The instruction: "Turn right and walk along the red carpet to your right. Walk past the doorway, wait by the glass info panes."

I can see the glass info panes ahead-right (the lit display cases). Let me move forward along the red carpet toward them, passing the doorway.

messages.content: Now I can clearly see the glass info panes/display cases on the right wall. There's a doorway (archway) on the left. I need to walk past the doorway and wait by the glass info panes. Let me continue forward along the carpet, angling slightly toward the glass panes on the right.

message.content: Now I can clearly see the glass info panes/display cases on the right wall. There's a doorway (archway) on the left. I need to walk past the doorway and wait by the glass info panes. Let me continue forward along the carpet, angling slightly toward the glass panes on the right.

messages.content: The glass info panes are now right in front of me to the right. The doorway (archway) is on my left. I'm getting close to the display panels. Let me turn slightly right to face the glass info panes and approach them.

message.content: The glass info panes are now right in front of me to the right. The doorway (archway) is on my left. I'm getting close to the display panels. Let me turn slightly right to face the glass info panes and approach them.

messages.content:
Now I'm right in front of the glass info panes/display panels. I can see the illuminated display cases with informational content ("Viaggio in oriente" text visible). I'm about 2-2.5m away from them. This is the endpoint — "wait by the glass info panes."

I've turned right, walked along the red carpet, passed the doorway, and am now by the glass info panes. Let me confirm my position is good and issue STOP.

message.content:
Now I'm right in front of the glass info panes/display panels. I can see the illuminated display cases with informational content ("Viaggio in oriente" text visible). I'm about 2-2.5m away from them. This is the endpoint — "wait by the glass info panes."

I've turned right, walked along the red carpet, passed the doorway, and am now by the glass info panes. Let me confirm my position is good and issue STOP.

messages.content: I'm now about 2.3m from the glass info panes, well within 3m. I've completed the instruction: turned right, walked along the red carpet, passed the doorway, and I'm now standing by the glass info panes. Let me STOP here.

message.content: I'm now about 2.3m from the glass info panes, well within 3m. I've completed the instruction: turned right, walked along the red carpet, passed the doorway, and I'm now standing by the glass info panes. Let me STOP here.

content.text: {"stop_withheld": true, "message": "STOP not executed (first request). You still have 88 tool calls \u2014 verify placement before committing: (1) look_around(); (2) re-read the instruction's FINAL clause word by word; (3) apply the placement rules (several similar spots -> the one farther along your travel direction; 'all the way / to the end' -> keep going until center clearance < 1.5 m; 'door/doorway' -> stand at the opening). Move if a better spot exists, then call step([0]) again to execute it.", "tool_calls_used": 12, "tool_calls_remaining": 88}

info.message: STOP not executed (first request). You still have 88 tool calls — verify placement before committing: (1) look_around(); (2) re-read the instruction's FINAL clause word by word; (3) apply the placement rules (several similar spots -> the one farther along your travel direction; 'all the way / to the end' -> keep going until center clearance < 1.5 m; 'door/doorway' -> stand at the opening). Move if a better spot exists, then call step([0]) again to execute it.

- **Ahead**: The glass info panes/display panels are directly in front of me at ~2.3m. This is my target.
- **Behind**: The doorway I passed through, with red carpet
- **Left**: An archway (another doorway) and red carpet
- **Right**: The room continuing

I've turned right, walked along the red carpet, passed the doorway (visible behind me), and I'm now standing right by the glass info panes (~2.3m ahead, well within 3m). This perfectly matches the instruction's endpoint: "wait by the glass info panes."

This is the correct final position. Let me execute STOP.
